title: Beehiiv vs GetResponse (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Beehiiv and GetResponse. See pricing, features, ratings, and find which email marketing tool suits your needs.
h1: Beehiiv vs GetResponse: Detailed Feature & Pricing Comparison
intro: Beehiiv and GetResponse are both popular email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences and use cases. Beehiiv specializes in newsletter publishing with monetization features, while GetResponse offers a broader marketing suite including webinars and automation. This guide helps you determine which platform is the better fit for your specific needs.

comparison_table.tool_b: Email, automation, webinars, landing pages, courses

choose_a_if: You're building a newsletter and want to monetize it through ads and referrals
You prioritize simplicity and a focused feature set for email publishing
You need to manage larger subscriber bases on a free plan (2,500 vs 500)
choose_b_if: You need a complete marketing platform beyond email (webinars, landing pages, courses)
You're budget-conscious and want the lowest-cost entry point ($19 vs $39)
You're an online course creator or webinar host looking for integrated tools

faq.a: Neither is universally 'better'—it depends on your use case. Beehiiv excels for newsletter monetization and publishing, with higher user ratings (4.7 on G2). GetResponse is better for those needing webinars, courses, and marketing automation all-in-one. Choose based on your primary business model.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Beehiiv or GetResponse?
faq.a: GetResponse is cheaper at $19/month for paid plans, compared to Beehiiv's $39/month. However, Beehiiv offers a more generous free tier (2,500 vs 500 subscribers), which may eliminate costs longer for smaller publishers.

faq.q: Can I switch from GetResponse to Beehiiv?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support standard email list exports and imports. Most email marketing tools allow CSV or API-based data migration, though you may lose some automation workflows. Plan your transition carefully to maintain subscriber engagement during the switch.
